hero.subheading: A data-driven email marketing system engineered to convert subscribers into compounding, channel-owned revenue.

background.title: Owned Channel Dominance
description: Mutimer is a Melbourne-based fashion brand with 88.6K Instagram followers and a 10,000-strong email list — half of whom had never made a purchase. They were almost entirely dependent on social media and rising paid ad costs. Ideom was brought in to build a repeatable, owned-channel revenue engine using Klaviyo email marketing.
background.quote: Ideom didn't just set up flows — they engineered a system that turns subscribers into customers on autopilot. The results speak for themselves.

challenges.title: Challenges
description: A 10,000-subscriber list with 50% never having purchased — untapped revenue sitting idle.
No automated flows or consistent campaign strategy in Klaviyo.
Almost total reliance on Instagram and paid ads, with rising costs and shadow-banning risk.
Customers heard from the brand sporadically, leading to low retention and loyalty.

solutions.title: Solutions
description: Built a complete Klaviyo automation stack — Welcome Series, Abandoned Browse/Cart/Checkout, Post-Purchase, and Win-Back flows — generating over $100K passively.
Designed an exclusivity and scarcity-driven campaign framework that leveraged Mutimer's high-demand drops to drive urgency and FOMO.
Instituted a VIP tier for 3rd-time buyers, granting early access and free shipping — turning loyal customers into brand advocates.
Deployed a multi-teaser campaign approach (up to 4 teasers pre-drop) to build anticipation and prime the list before every major release.

emailSequence.title: Campaigns that converted at scale
description: 1. We leveraged exclusivity and scarcity — Mutimer frequently sold out, and we weaponised that.
2. We ran up to 4 teaser campaigns before each major drop to build anticipation and prime the list.
3. VIP customers (3rd purchase+) received early access 30–60 minutes before anyone else.
4. We kept every email on-brand: high-quality imagery, simple copy, and one clear CTA.
The goal was always the same: make every email feel like a privilege to receive.

thoughtProcess.title: The campaign philosophy
content: We had one core principle: email should feel like getting a tip from a friend, not a broadcast from a brand.
Mutimer's audience was already engaged on Instagram — we needed to translate that energy into the inbox.
• teaser emails built genuine anticipation without overpromising
• VIP access made loyal customers feel seen and rewarded
• and that every drop email was so clean and simple that clicking was the only logical action
The launch email is not the place to be clever. Get them to the link. That's the whole job.
We also protected deliverability at every step — warming the domain carefully and monitoring open rates across ISPs.
The result: 57.99% average open rate across all campaigns. Industry average is 20–25%.

emails.label: Drop Tease #1
explanation: The opening salvo — designed to signal that something big was coming.
High-quality product photography with minimal copy. Pure visual desire.
No price. No CTA. Just anticipation.
This set the tone: Mutimer doesn't beg for attention. It commands it.
Sent to the full list to maximise reach and establish the drop in every inbox.

emails.label: Drop Tease #2
explanation: The second teaser provided a date — giving subscribers something concrete to hold onto.
We paired the date reveal with more product imagery to sustain momentum.
This email also began qualifying intent: who opened, who clicked, who was primed to buy.
Open rates at this stage consistently exceeded 60%, confirming the list was warm and ready.
The energy from the first email was fully maintained — no drop-off in engagement.

emails.label: Drop Tease #3 & #4
explanation: Final teasers in the sequence — focused on urgency and last-chance prep.
We introduced the VIP early-access window here, creating a two-tier launch moment.
Tease #4 subject line: 'Dec 9th - Your Early Access' — achieved a 70% open rate.
By this point the list was conditioned: when Mutimer emails, you open it immediately.
These emails required almost no copywriting — the brand equity did the heavy lifting.

emails.label: Launch Email
explanation: The most simple email in the entire sequence — by design.
Subject: 'REDUX Is Here'. Open rate: 56.94%. Revenue from this single email: $74,237.90.
One image. One link. One job: get them to the store.
No countdown timers. No urgency copy. The product sold itself.
This is the proof: when you do the pre-work right, the launch email is almost effortless.

results.label: Email Attributed Revenue
results.desc: 52.42% of all revenue driven by email

testimonials.quote: Ideom didn't just set up flows — they engineered a system that turns subscribers into customers on autopilot. The results speak for themselves.
